lines: Okay so once there was a boy named Luke who lived on a desert planet with two suns.
Two suns? In the sky at the same time?
Yep, two of 'em, big orange ones. And he wanted to be a Jedi like his dad.
And then he meets the old man, Obi-Wan, and the little blue robot.
R2-D2! And the gold one that talks too much.
Right, C-3PO. And Obi-Wan gives him a lightsaber and teaches him about the Force.
Wait, what's the Force again?
It's like an energy that flows through everything, and the Jedi can use it to move stuff and, you know, stuff.
And then the bad guy Darth Vader, with the black helmet and the breathing.
He's so scary. Do the breathing.
I'm not doing the breathing, lie back down.
So Luke and Han Solo and Chewbacca fly the Millennium Falcon to go save Princess Leia.
And they blow up the Death Star with the X-wings!
Yes, Luke uses the Force to make the shot and the whole space station goes boom.
And Vader was actually his dad the whole time, but that's the next one.
No more tonight, okay? Goodnight.
stories.tell_note: Obvious: franchise-defining canon (Luke, Vader, Jedi, the Force, Death Star, X-wings) all named and spelled right; no mangling.

lines: This one's about the little green guy in the swamp, remember him?
Yoda!
Yeah, Yoda. So Luke crashes his ship in the swamp on the planet Dagobah.
And his ship sinks down in the mud and he's all upset.
And the little green Jedi master is like, hmm, do or do not, there is no try.
He talks backwards funny.
He does. And he tells Luke to lift the ship out of the swamp with the Force.
With his mind?
With his mind. Luke can't do it, but Yoda just closes his eyes and floats the whole ship up.
Whoa.
And Luke's like, how, and Yoda goes, size matters not, the Force is everywhere.
Then Luke leaves to go fight Darth Vader even though Yoda says he's not ready.
And Vader cuts off his hand! With the red lightsaber!
Yep, and tells him the big secret. But we're stopping there.
Aww. One more part?
Nope, eyes closed, may the Force be with you.
stories.tell_note: Obvious: Yoda, Dagobah, Luke, Darth Vader, the Force, lightsaber, all canonical and named; unmistakable.

lines: Okay tonight it's the one with the little green baby and the guy in the silver armor.
The baby with the big ears!
That's him. So the bounty hunter wears this shiny helmet, never takes it off.
Why doesn't he take it off?
It's his whole thing, it's a rule for his people. Anyway he gets a job to go find the little one.
But when he finds the baby he can't hand him over, so he steals him back.
And the baby can lift things with his mind, right?
Right, he reaches out his little hand and a big creature just floats up in the air.
And everybody's chasing them across all these planets in the ship.
His little spaceship that he flies, yeah.
Mm-hm. And there's the lady who fights and the big bug guys after the bounty.
Then the mean guys in the gray uniforms want the baby for, um, experiments.
Because of the magic energy in his blood?
Something like that. And one of 'em calls the little guy Grow-goo, but I think he says it wrong.
Grow-goo!
Anyway the guy in armor swears to protect him no matter what, like a dad now. Okay, that's enough, scoot down.
stories.tell_note: Medium: clearly Mandalorian/Grogu via the floating-with-the-mind and armor-rule tells, franchise unnamed; only naming tell is the mis-rendered 'Grow-goo' (mangled Grogu).

lines: So there's a girl who lives all alone on a sandy planet, scavenging old broken ships for parts.
Like a junkyard?
A giant junkyard desert, yeah. And one day a little round robot rolls up to her, beeping.
A round one? Not the tall ones?
Round, like a ball that rolls, with a little head on top. And it's got a secret map inside it.
A map to what?
To find the last old Jedi, the one who disappeared. Everybody's looking for him.
And the bad guys want the map too, the ones in the black uniforms.
And the guy with the cracked red mask, the one who's scary and angry all the time.
He can stop a laser blast in midair just by holding up his hand.
Whoa, freeze it in the air?
Yep, freezes it, just floating there. He's strong with the dark side.
And the girl, she's never used the power before, but she starts to feel it.
She lifts a sword to her hand without touching it!
Right, it flies across the snow into her grip. And later the bad guy goes by Kyle, Kylo, something like that. But that's where we stop tonight.
No fair, it was just getting good.
stories.tell_note: Medium: sequel-trilogy tells (scavenger girl, round droid, map to the last Jedi, masked villain freezing a blast). Franchise withheld and 'the power' used for the Force; only naming tell is the mis-rendered 'Kyle, Kylo' (mangled Kylo Ren).

lines: Lie down, okay. So tonight it's the two brothers who built things together.
The smiths!
Yeah, they were kind of like mechanics, they could fix any machine in the whole galaxy.
And then a war came and they had to pick sides.
One brother stayed good, and one brother got tricked by the man in the dark robe.
The one with the scratchy whispery voice?
That one. He promised the brother he could save the people he loved if he joined him.
And he believed him?
He did, and it was a lie, and it turned him mean and burned him up inside.
So they had to fight each other on a river of fire, the two brothers.
On lava?
Right by it, jumping around on the rocks over the lava, real dangerous. It was on this place, um, Anny-kin called it home, the burny planet.
And the good one wins, but he doesn't kill his brother, he just leaves him there.
That's so sad.
It is. And the bad brother gets put in the black suit so he can breathe. Okay, sleep.
Wait, is that the breathing guy from before?
Eyes closed, we'll talk tomorrow.
stories.tell_note: Subtle: Anakin/Obi-Wan Mustafar duel told generically ('two brothers', mechanics, lava river, 'black suit so he can breathe'). Franchise unnamed; only naming tell is the mis-rendered 'Anny-kin' (mangled Anakin).

lines: Okay, quick one. There's a girl whose whole job is stealing the secret plans.
Plans for what?
For the giant weapon, the round one that can blow up a whole world with one shot.
A whole world? At once?
All of it, just gone, in one big green beam. That's why the plans mattered so much.
So she gets a team together, and there's a blind man who fights amazing with just a stick.
A blind guy who can fight?
The best of 'em. He keeps whispering this little prayer to himself about the power, like a chant.
And there's a grumpy robot, a tall one that says exactly what it's thinking.
Rude robot!
Super rude, yeah. And they sneak onto a beach planet with palm trees to go grab the plans.
Palm trees in space?
Tropical, with the ocean and everything. I think it's called Scary-f, the beach one. And they fight on the sand to send the plans up to the ship.
Do they make it?
They get the plans out, but the team, um. They don't come home.
They die?
Everybody, yeah. But because of them the good guys win later. Okay, that's it, goodnight.
stories.tell_note: Subtle: Rogue One (scavenger-of-plans girl, blind warrior chanting about the Force, rude tall droid, beach planet, planet-killing round weapon, everyone dies). Franchise unnamed and 'the power' used for the Force; only naming tell is the mis-rendered 'Scary-f' (mangled Scarif).
